title: Basic Photo Editing
drDigitalIntro:
You can improve photos without being a pro.
• This photo came out way too dark — let's fix it.
• Drag the Brightness slider to make it lighter.
• Drag the Contrast slider to sharpen the colors.
• You can also Rotate, Crop, or Revert to undo all changes.

steps.say: Click the Bird in Garden photo to open it.
This photo is too dark! Drag the Brightness slider up to about 90-110%.
Now drag the Contrast slider up to about 90-110% to sharpen the colors.
Click Rotate to turn the photo 90 degrees.
Try a different crop — click Square to crop it square.
Click Revert to undo all changes and restore the original.

drDigitalSuccess: You fixed a dark photo and tried all the editing tools! The original is always preserved — you can always Revert.
drDigitalHint: Open the photo first, then use the Brightness and Contrast sliders to fix it. Revert undoes all changes.
